checks.description: AST-parse hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py; locate the assignment to CP_AGENTS; assert its value is a set literal containing the exact string member 'test-writer'. Fallback regex acceptable ONLY if AST parse fails: CP_AGENTS\s*=\s*\{[^}]*["']test-writer["'][^}]*\} (multiline, set-body-anchored).
AST-parse hooks/prompt-workflow.py; locate the assignment to agent_types; assert its value is a list literal containing the exact string member 'test-writer'.

shell_token_check.description: Within the fallback `for agent in ... ; do` loop wordlist (around line 133-136), with shell comments stripped, the wordlist tokens MUST include 'test-writer' as an exact whitespace-separated shell word — NOT as 'not-test-writer', NOT as a `# test-writer` comment, NOT as a substring of another token

scoped_checks.description: Within procedure step 5 paragraph: per-task path MUST be cited; the unqualified 'Write tests/generated/manifest.json' wording (backticked or not) MUST NOT appear unless explicitly annotated '(global)' or '(index)'

Within the Output JSON Report code-fence section: manifest_path field MUST be per-task; MUST NOT be the global path; the global-index shape kind=index is referenced (either as the strict JSON form or the prose annotation)

Inside Phase 5: step 1 reads per-task manifest (not global), pytest is scoped to per-task dir, the LEGITIMATE global-index presence sentinel must remain (regex requires the explicit literal 'tests/generated/manifest.json is missing' inside the test_writer_expected sentinel paragraph), AND the Cycle-1-drift active_tests-from-global phrasing MUST NOT appear
Inside the manifest_verification schema block: manifest_path field MUST be per-task; the global path MUST NOT remain as the schema's manifest_path value
Inside Step 11 QA dispatch: a per-task manifest-path line MUST appear (REQUIRED — not just unqualified-global FORBIDDEN; ensures the QA dispatch actually carries the per-task path)
Inside the Dev-handoff text (~line 666): per-task manifest path MUST appear; unqualified global path MUST NOT appear (annotated forms acceptable)

args: set -eu; PATHS=$(git ls-tree -r HEAD --name-only -- agent-scores.json agent-scores.json.lock .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/); FAIL=0; MISSING=""; while IFS= read -r p; do [ -z "$p" ] && continue; if [ ! -e "$p" ]; then MISSING="$MISSING $p"; FAIL=1; fi; done <<< "$PATHS"; if [ "$FAIL" -ne 0 ]; then echo "MISSING_WORKING_TREE:$MISSING" >&2; fi; exit $FAIL

assertions.description: For EVERY path previously tracked at HEAD under the three targets, the working-tree copy MUST still exist (test `-e`). Content drift is NOT checked — cp-state-qa.json and agent-scores.json are inherently mutable runtime telemetry that drifts from HEAD before dev acts (this is the OBJ-11 fix; QA iter-2 verified empirical drift).

hook_check._scope_label: DERIVED_WORK: necessary side-effect of M-P2.3 .gitignore append on already-tracked files; not a separate user-stated requirement. Cross-reference: out_of_scope_observation #1 in ba-qa-report-20260520-221452.json flags orchestrator awareness.
hook_check._obj11_note: iter-3 replaces prior per_path_survival_and_content_match (SHA equality) with per_path_existence_only because cp-state-qa.json and agent-scores.json are mutable runtime telemetry that drifts from HEAD even before dev acts — SHA equality would unfairly fail compliant dev work. The intentional tradeoff: content-destruction is not detected; OBJ-11 codex review accepts this as the only safe ground-truth without a pre-dev baseline.

checks.description: AST-parse regen_readme.py; locate the SKIP_NAMES assignment; assert the set literal value (via ast.literal_eval) contains BOTH 'agent-scores.json' AND 'agent-scores.json.lock' as string members (order-independent set membership, not regex)
AST-parse tree.py; locate the SKIP_FILES assignment; assert the set literal contains BOTH literal members

args:
set -eu; ( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && PYTHONPATH="$PWD" python3 - <<'PY'
import tempfile, pathlib
from hooks.doc_sync import tree as tree_mod
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
    root = pathlib.Path(td)
    claude_dir = root / '.claude'
    # positive leak path — must be SUPPRESSED after dev fix
    leak = claude_dir / 'specs' / 'spec-foo'
    leak.mkdir(parents=True)
    (leak / 'cp-state.json').write_text('{}')
    # negative control — a legitimate non-.claude/specs subtree that ALSO contains a 'specs' segment;
    # if dev's fix is a blanket i.name == 'specs' predicate, this would also be suppressed (substitutability defect).
    # Correct path-aware fix must preserve this.
    ordinary = claude_dir / 'ordinary' / 'specs'
    ordinary.mkdir(parents=True)
    (ordinary / 'keep.txt').write_text('z')
    # control file proving build_tree actually ran
    (claude_dir / 'normal.txt').write_text('y')
    lines = tree_mod.build_tree(claude_dir)
    out = '\n'.join(lines)
    # Positive control: build_tree ran
    assert 'normal.txt' in out, f'build_tree did not include control file normal.txt: {out!r}'
    # Negative control: legitimate non-.claude/specs path with a specs segment MUST survive
    assert 'keep.txt' in out, f'build_tree suppressed legitimate ordinary/specs/keep.txt — dev fix is too broad (likely i.name == specs blanket): {out!r}'
    assert 'ordinary/' in out, f'build_tree suppressed legitimate ordinary/ — dev fix is too broad: {out!r}'
    # Positive leak suppression: the .claude/specs/spec-foo/cp-state.json leak path MUST be excluded.
    # Use exact tree-rendered tokens so a substring like 'ordinary/specs/' does NOT trigger the suppression assertion.
    assert 'spec-foo/' not in out, f'build_tree did NOT suppress leak-path spec-foo/: {out!r}'
    assert 'cp-state.json' not in out, f'build_tree did NOT suppress leak-path cp-state.json: {out!r}'
    # The token 'specs/' appears in BOTH ordinary/specs/ and .claude/specs/ — assert the leak path does not surface
    # by requiring the rendered output's specs/ occurrences to be reachable only via ordinary/.
    # Concretely: there must be no specs/ child immediately under the .claude/ root (depth-1 tree position).
    # tree.py renders depth-1 children at the top with a connector and no prefix; thus a top-level 'specs/' line would be '\u2502   ' or no-prefix line ending with 'specs/'.
    # Robust check: split output into lines; assert no line is exactly '\u2514\u2500\u2500 specs/' or '\u251c\u2500\u2500 specs/' (top-level connector + specs/).
    bad_lines = [ln for ln in lines if ln.endswith('\u2500\u2500 specs/') and '\u2502' not in ln[:ln.rindex('\u2500\u2500 ')] and not ln.startswith('    ')]
    # Simpler equivalent: top-level lines have no leading spaces or pipes — they begin with the connector glyph.
    top_level_lines = [ln for ln in lines if not (ln.startswith(' ') or ln.startswith('\u2502') or ln.startswith('    '))]
    assert not any(ln.endswith(' specs/') for ln in top_level_lines), f'top-level specs/ child of .claude NOT suppressed: top_level={top_level_lines!r}'
    print('PASS: leak path suppressed; negative control preserved; control file present')
PY
)

assertions.description: Runtime fixture: PYTHONPATH=$PWD allows the package-relative import (from hooks.doc_sync import tree as tree_mod). build_tree(root/'.claude') exercises the ACTUAL leak path (specs/ as direct child of .claude is NOT dot-prefixed, so today's startswith('.') filter does not suppress it). The fixture asserts (i) control file normal.txt present, (ii) negative-control ordinary/specs/keep.txt preserved (forces path-aware predicate, NOT blanket i.name=='specs'), (iii) leak tokens spec-foo/ and cp-state.json absent, (iv) no top-level specs/ child of .claude. Any pass-as-substitute fix (e.g., adding 'specs' to IGNORE_DIRS) would FAIL on assertion (ii). Any vacuous fix (no actual code change) would FAIL on assertions (iii) and (iv).

hook_check._obj12_note: iter-3 replaces prior specs_exclusion_runtime check that had (a) HARD ImportError from importlib.util loading tree.py outside its package context (relative import `from .extract`), and (b) VACUOUS test (build_tree(root) where .claude is dot-prefixed and already excluded). New form: PYTHONPATH=$PWD + package import + build_tree(root/'.claude') to exercise the actual leak. Negative-control fixture (ordinary/specs/keep.txt) prevents the substitutability defect of a blanket name predicate per codex iter-3 review.

shell_aware_check.description: After shell-comment stripping AND quoted-string masking, every line containing python3 as an executable command MUST be inside a subshell that first sources the venv. Inline `# python3` comments and "python3" literal strings do not count.

hook_check._verification_algorithm: (1) For each file: read all lines. (2) Strip everything from the first unquoted '#' on each line. (3) Mask all contents between matching unescaped single/double quotes (replace with empty placeholder) so 'python3' inside a string literal does NOT count as an invocation. (4) After both preprocessings, find every remaining line containing the bare token 'python3' (\bpython3\b). (5) For each such line, assert the original (pre-masked) line matches the canonical regex `\(\s*source\s+(?:~|\$\{?HOME\}?|/root)/\.claude/venv/bin/activate\s*&&\s*python3\b`. If any python3 invocation site (post-strip-and-mask) does NOT satisfy on its line, AC-10 fails with the file:line cited.

checks.description: FILE-WIDE: no decimal Sub-step 12.N label remains anywhere
WITHIN Step 12 section only: a cross-reference to 'spec section updates' (replacing former 'Sub-step 12.0') MUST exist as integer-step prose
WITHIN the '## Mascot Score Changes' summary section: the cross-reference MUST anchor the score-update events to 'Step 12' (replacing former 'Sub-step 12.1')

paragraph_scope.description: Slice from the NEW-exemption anchor heading to (whichever comes first): a blank line, the next bold-heading paragraph marker, or the next section heading

paragraph_must_contain_all_regex.regex: 见习学徒|初级工匠|熟练工匠|资深工匠|宗师级
paragraph_must_contain_all_regex.description: (b) At least one of the five spec §5.1 rank-label samples
(d) Re-affirmation that BOTH code comments AND stderr/diagnostic strings remain English-only — must be on the BOTH-sides clause, not a single-sided one

hook_check._anti_substitution_doc: All four assertions are scoped to the NEW exemption paragraph (paragraph-level, not section-level). The existing 'Exemption (verbatim user-binding quotes)' paragraph at line ~320 cannot satisfy these because the paragraph-scope anchor key requires the 'Cycle-2 user-facing' phrase.
